data.title: Changchun High-Tech Industry Unit Gets China Nod For Clinical Trial of IBD Drug
data.description:
Changchun High-Tech Industry Group's unit, Changchun Jinsai Pharmaceutical, obtained clinical trial approval in China for its GenSci120 injection, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The drug will be tested as treatment for inflammatory bowel diseases, including ulcerative colitis and Crohn's disease, the pharmaceutical company said.

The company's shares rose around 2% in recent trade.

data.title: VRL Logistics shares soar 20% amid heavy volumes, profit jumps 4x in Q3FY25
data.description:
Shares of VRL Logistics skyrocketed 20 percent to Rs 559 apiece on February 6 after the company posted strong Q3FY25 results. The stock saw heavy trading volumes in early sessions, with approximately 8 lakh shares changing hands on the NSE and BSE—significantly higher than the one-week average of 66,000 shares across both exchanges.

Despite this rally, the stock has remained largely stagnant over the past three months, slipping 3 percent, compared to the Nifty 50’s 1 percent decline.

Catch all the market action on our LIVE blog

VRL Logistics reported a 12 percent YoY increase in revenue to Rs 830 crore in Q3FY25, while net profit soared 4x YoY to Rs 59 crore. Operating performance also showed significant improvement, with EBITDA rising 78 percent YoY to ₹172 crore. Margins expanded by a sharp 800 basis points YoY to 21 percent.

The company attributed margin expansion to the successful implementation of freight hikes, which boosted realisations across all sectors and geographies while maintaining tonnage.

Further efficiencies came from route optimization, reducing reliance on multiple transshipment hubs along major corridors. This minimized frequent loading and unloading of consignments, while better mileage and load factors for company-owned vehicles improved utilization—ultimately driving up margins.

VRL Logistics continued its expansion, increasing its branch count from 1,209 in FY24 to 1,248 in 9MFY25, with a net addition of 39 branches (64 new, 25 closed).

The company also ramped up capital expenditure, with Rs 276.05 crore spent in Q3FY25 and Rs 395.46 crore in 9MFY25. Key investments included property acquisitions in Bengaluru, Mysuru, and Mangaluru. Meanwhile, its long-term ICRA credit rating remains stable at A+ (Positive).Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: PREVIEW: Indian clothing retailer Trent falls ahead of Q3 results
data.description:
** Shares of clothing retailer Trent fall 1.3% to 5,675 rupees ahead of Q3 results

** Analysts expect TREN to report ~33% rise in Q3 revenue, its slowest pace of growth since March 2021 - LSEG data

** Analysts expect 23.7% rise in Q3 profit, slowest in six quarters

** Co's store expansion for popular Zudio brand has moderated, analysts said

** TREN likely to fall short of meeting its fiscal-year target of 200 Zudio store additions in a muted demand environment- analysts

** Shares of Trent have fallen about 19.2% YTD, after more than doubling in value in 2023 and 2024

** Stock rated 'buy' on avg by 18 analysts, avg PT of 6,750.6 rupees implies a ~18.3% upside to current price

data.title: Fosun Pharmaceutical Gains EU Marketing Approval for Lung Cancer Drug
data.description:
Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical secured EU marketing authorization for its PD-1 inhibitor, Serplulimab, in combination with carboplatin and etoposide for treating extensive-stage small cell lung cancer.

The approval grants the drug centralized marketing rights across all EU member states, Iceland, Liechtenstein and Norway, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Shares of the pharnaceutical company rose 1% in recent trade on the Shanghai bourse.

data.title: U.S. Dollar Likely to Remain Under Pressure Vs. Yen, Charts Show — Market Talk
data.description: The U.S. dollar is likely to stay under pressure versus the yen, based on charts, says Quek Ser Leang at UOB's Global Economics & Markets Research in a research report. The currency pair's latest price movements have resulted in a swift build-up in weekly downward momentum, the markets strategist notes. The currency pair breaking below the bottom of the weekly Ichimoku cloud at 151.10 is likely to trigger further sharp losses, the strategist adds. Subsequent meaningful support level would be at December 2024's low of 148.63. USD/JPY is 0.3% lower at 152.20. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Lithium Resources Signs Heritage Protection Agreement for Western Australian Gold Project
data.description:
Global Lithium Resources signed a heritage protection agreement for the Marble Bar Gold Project in West Australia with the Nyamal Aboriginal Corporation, the company said in a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The mining firm will conduct proactive engagements with the Aboriginal corporation and the Nyamal traditional owners to help protect heritage and native title rights within the recently expanded Marble Bar project in Western Australia.

Company shares were up 2% in recent trade.

data.title: SGX Sicom Benchmark March TSR20 Rubber Up 2.8 Cents at 195.8 U.S. Cents/Kg
SGX Sicom Benchmark March RSS3 Rubber Up 1.2 Cents at 240.5 U.S. Cents/Kg
Mitsubishi Chemical Hldgs Sees FY Net Y52.00B
Mitsubishi Chemical Hldgs 9-Mos Net Y59.37B Vs Net Y103.86B
Arab National Bank Posts 2024 Earnings Beat; Dividend Slips
data.description:
Arab National Bank (SASE:1080) closed Wednesday trading 0.37% in the red as the board proposed a lower dividend despite bumper and better-than-expected 2024 results from the Saudi Arabian lender.

In total, the board intends to distribute 1.30 billion Saudi riyals, or 0.65 riyal per share, to shareholders for the second half of 2024. Together with the interim dividend, the bank would pay out 1.30 riyals per share for the year, down from 1.35 riyals in 2023.

In contrast, the company booked a 22% year-over-year surge in 2024 net profit to 4.97 billion riyals as impairment charges fell and income climbed. The FactSet-compiled consensus estimate for net income was 4.86 billion riyals.

Meanwhile, both financing and investment income increased during the year, helping push the group's net special commission income nearly 10% higher over the period.

Somewhat weighing on the performance, on the other hand, were higher costs related to general and administrative expenses and lower net gains or losses from non-trading instruments, net other operating income and net exchange income.

As of Dec. 31, 2024, the Tadawul-listed bank, one of the biggest in the region, has registered assets worth 248.31 billion riyals and investments clocking in at 52.35 billion riyals.

data.title: India's ITC slips ahead of Q3 results
data.description:
** Shares of consumer goods maker ITC fall 1.1% ahead of third-quarter results

** Analysts' on average expect co to post a 7.3% Y/Y drop in Q3 profit at 51.90 billion rupees ($592.9 million) - LSEG data

** Consumer goods giants have been grappling with food inflation and moderation in urban consumption with gradual recovery in rural markets, said analysts at Nirmal Bang

** Rival Nestle India missed Q3 profit view on slowdown in consumer spending, while Hindustan Unilever said it expects near-term margins at lower end of its previous forecast range of 23%-24%

** Analysts covering ITC on avg have a "buy" rating on stock, same as on HLL, while NEST rated "hold" on avg - LSEG

** Stock had shed ~7% in Q3

($1 = 87.5300 Indian rupees)

data.title: Macquarie hikes PT for Amcor's Australian shares
data.description:
** Macquarie raises target price for Amcor's Australian shares to A$18.20 from A$18.00

** Stock down 0.1% at A$16.15 after rising to a nine-week high on Wednesday

** Packaging co reaffirmed its FY25 EPS outlook on Tuesday

** Brokerage Macquarie takes into account outlook reaffirmation and strong first-quarter earnings of Berry Group , a company Amcor agreed to buy in November

** Seven of 12 analysts rate the stock "buy" or higher, 4 "hold" and 1 "sell"; their median PT is A$17.20 – LSEG data

** Stock rose 5.3% in 2024

data.title: Australia's TMK Energy soars on upbeat gas production results
data.description:
** Shares of TMK Energy rise 25% to A$0.0025

** The gas exploration co says its Gurvantes XXXV Coal Seam Gas project reported around 330% increase in total gas produced in January 2025 compared to prior month

** Co adds project's total gas produced was ~40% increaseover the previous monthly record

** Stock flat in 2025 so far

data.title: CBOT soybeans biased to retest support at $10.53-1/2
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean March contract (SH25) is biased to retest support at $10.53-1/2 per bushel, a break below could open the way towards the $10.37-1/2 to $10.44-1/4 range.

The correction triggered by resistance at $10.79-1/2 looks so deep that it suggests the formation of a top. It is doubtful that the market is riding on a powerful wave C, which is supposed to travel to $11.12.

In the meantime, it could be too early to conclude that the uptrend from $9.47 has reversed, as the contract still hovers above a rising trendline.

Signals will be much clearer when the contract breaks above $10.69-3/4 or below $10.44-1/4. On the daily chart, the contract has broken support at $10.64, after failing to break resistance at $10.17-1/4.

A fall toward $10.41-1/2 seems to be much easier choice for the market, instead of retesting $10.79-1/4.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Cullen Resources Identifies Gold Prospects at Western Australia Project, Shares Surge 50%
data.description:
Cullen Resources identified gold prospects at the Yardilla gold project in Western Australia, characterized by substantial gold-in-calcrete soil anomalies, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The project contains the significant Lila and Cleanthes gold prospects, defined by gold-in-calcrete soil anomalies over 14 parts-per-billion to 86 ppb grade of gold from historical, systematic exploration.

Other historical exploration outlined gold soil anomalies at Lila and Lila West, using a mobile metal ion assaying method, and at the Ten Mile Rocks prospect using bulk leach extractable gold of drainage samples.

Cullen Exploration, a wholly owned unit of Cullen Resources, applied for the adjoining ground to its tenement in Western Australia, which is not part of the option-to-purchase.

The unit is in talks to acquire 90% of the exploration license application for the Yardilla project located at the state's Eastern Goldfields.

Proposed further work at the project includes reconnaissance rock and soil sampling, with mapping and drilling expected upon granting of Cullen's tenement.

The mining firm's shares surged 50% in recent trading on Thursday.

data.title: Market Chatter: China Slams US for 'Irresponsible' Remarks on Panama Canal
data.description:
Beijing filed complaints with Washington over "irresponsible" comments and "attacks" on China's partnership with Panama regarding the Panama Canal, Reuters reported Wednesday.

US Secretary of State Marco Rubio warned that China's influence over the Panama Canal threatened its neutrality and violated the Treaty governing its operation. Rubio also lauded Panama for allowing its participation in China's Belt and Road Initiative to expire.

China's foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said China's cooperation with Panama under the Belt and Road Initiative remains on track, despite Panama's recent decision to let its participation in the infrastructure program lapse, according to the report.

Lin urged all parties to resist external interference and focus on long-term bilateral interests, Reuters wrote.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Petratherm Encounters High-Grade Heavy Mineral Sand Discovery at South Australia Project; Shares Down 20%
data.description:
Petratherm encountered a high-grade heavy mineral sand discovery during a recent drill program at the Rosewood prospect in South Australia, according to a Thursday filing with the Australian bourse.

The new intersections include 28 meters at 13.6% heavy minerals from a depth of 10 meters and 16 meters at 12.1% heavy minerals from 14 meters.

The company expects results from other Muckanippie Project heavy minerals targets within two weeks, the filing said.

Petratherm's shares plunged 20% in recent Thursday trade.

data.title: ASX-listed Resouro Strategic gains on positive results from Brazil project
data.description:
** ASX-listed shares of Resouro Strategic Metals rise 14.6% to A$0.235, highest level since January 30

** Stock rose as much as 22% earlier in the day to A$0.250, marking its biggest percent gain since January 21

** Canada-based mineral explorer announces positive assay results finding high-grade titanium at its Tiros Titanium and Rare Earth Elements Project in Brazil

** Stock rose 6.8% YTD, including current session's moves

data.title: Welspun Corp Clocks Gains in Fiscal Q3 Consolidated Net Profit
data.description:
Welspun Corp recorded a rise in its consolidated attributable net profit to 6.75 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, against 2.92 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share climbed to 25.60 rupees compared with the year-ago figure of 11.13 rupees, according to a Wednesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges by the iron pipes manufacturer.

Revenue from operations in fiscal Q3 declined to 36.1 billion rupees from 47.5 billion rupees a year earlier.

Shares of the company were down over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Equitable Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Equitable Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of $1.57​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of $1.33. The mean expectation of fourteen analysts for the quarter was for earnings of $1.62 per share. Wall Street expected results to range from $1.58 to $1.68 per share.

  • Revenue rose 66.9% to $3.62 billion from a year ago; analysts expected $4.04 billion.

  • Equitable Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was $2.76​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $873 million.

  • Equitable Holdings Inc shares had risen by 12.9% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 2.2% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, six analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 10 "strong buy" or "buy," 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the diversified investment services peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Equitable Holdings Inc is $59.00

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 6 at 04:15 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Ørsted Lowers Investment Targets in Adjusted Business Plan
data.description:
Ørsted adjusted its business plan due to further pressure on its credit metrics from challenging market developments, particularly related to the US offshore wind portfolio.

The green energy company said Wednesday that it will now pursue an investment program of between 210 billion Danish kroner and 230 billion kroner from 2024 to 2030. This reflects a 25% reduction from its previous investment target, Reuters reported the same day.

Ørsted also withdrew its targets of achieving 35 gigawatts to 38 GW in installed renewable capacity by 2030 and 39 billion kroner to 43 billion kroner of EBITDA by 2030. The company added that this will not impact the 9 GW worth of renewable projects under construction.

Ørsted maintained its target of reinstating dividends from the 2026 financial year.

data.title: Malaysia's Industrial Sector Set for Sustained Growth, Says Knight Frank — Market Talk
data.description:
0417 GMT - Malaysia's industrial sector is set for sustained growth in 2025, supported by strategic government initiatives, infrastructure improvements and AI-powered industrial parks, Knight Frank says in a note. Amid global trade tensions, especially with Trump's tariff pledges, manufacturers are expected to shift their production to Southeast Asia, it adds. Malaysia's strategic location and evolving industrial ecosystem looks well-positioned to benefit from such a shift, it adds. AI and automation are transforming Malaysia's industrial landscape, enhancing operational efficiency and sustainability, Knight Frank notes. With strong investor interest in logistics, e-commerce and high-tech manufacturing, Malaysia could remain a preferred destination for industrial investments, with favorable policies and skilled workforce, it adds. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0358 GMT - The Singapore dollar slightly weakens against its U.S. counterpart in the Asian trading session on possible position adjustments. Market participants appear to be waiting for a phone call to take place between U.S. President Trump and China's President Xi, and whether a deal can be reached to lower or remove tariffs, say two members of CBA's Global Economic & Markets Research in a note. However, with Trump having said there is no hurry to talk to President Xi, the analysts note the longer the wait for the expected Trump-Xi call, the more likely market participants will lose hope of a trade deal between U.S. and China. USD/SGD is 0.1% higher at 1.3502. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0340 GMT - Taiwan's consumer prices likely rose 2.4% on year in January, according to the median forecast of six estimates polled by The Wall Street Journal. That compares with the 2.1% inflation print in December, and 2.08% in November. Economists think that inflation likely accelerated due to the Lunar New Year holiday falling in January this year against early February in 2024. Prices of food and services were notably higher during the holiday period. Looking ahead, DBS economists expect inflation to remain stable, driven by weak oil prices and a cooling property market. However, possible U.S. tariffs on oil, gas, semiconductors, among others, could weigh on consumer prices, they caution. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0320 GMT - The offshore yuan may consolidate around 7.3000 against the dollar, DBS Group Research's Chang Wei Liang says in commentary. "Given prospects of U.S.-China negotiations, trade tensions could stay contained for now," the foreign-exchange and credit strategist says. The markets appear to have taken the U.S. tariffs "in stride," as the levies seem to be within expectations, the strategist says. Also, China reinforced its commitment to yuan stability by holding the USD/CNY daily fixing at around 7.1700 on Wednesday, the strategist adds. The USD/CNY daily fixing was at 7.1693 on Wednesday and 7.1691 on Thursday. USD/CNH is 0.05% higher at 7.2836; USD/CNY is up 0.1% at 7.2820. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0234 GMT - Copper prices are higher in early Asian trade as investors closely monitored the impact of the U.S.-China trade war. After Chinese markets returned from the Lunar New Year holiday, investors appear to "take the rising trade tensions in their stride, with market activity relatively subdued," ANZ Research analysts say in a note. Investors are in search for any signs of a rebound in demand following the holidays amid a rise in supply, they add. The benchmark three-month LME contract is up 1.0% at $9,332.00 a ton. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0214 GMT - The yen strengthens against other G-10 and Asian currencies in the morning session, after a Bank of Japan board member's remarks boosted prospects of more rate hikes. BOJ policy board member Naoki Tamura told local leaders in Nagano, Japan, that there was a need to raise rates to at least around 1% in the latter half of FY 2025 and that neutral rates are probably around 1% at a minimum. At last month's meeting, the Bank of Japan raised its policy rate to around 0.5% from around 0.25% previously. USD/JPY is 0.4% lower at 151.99 after earlier touching 151.83, the lowest intraday level since Dec. 12. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0134 GMT - Indonesia's 1Q GDP growth is expected to remain steady, supported by increased government social spending that could boost household consumption, CIMB economists say in a note. The economists keep Indonesia's 2025 GDP growth estimate at 5.0%. With supportive growth and external uncertainties, Bank Indonesia is likely to take a wait-and-see approach, balancing stability and growth while strategically reducing the policy rate only when a favorable window arises, they say. CIMB maintains its forecast for one 25bps rate cut per quarter, bringing the policy rate to 5.00% by end-2025.(yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0126 GMT - Most JGBs edge higher in the morning Tokyo session, tracking price gains in U.S. Treasurys overnight. Both JGBs and Treasurys tend to move in tandem. Today's auction of 900 billion yen in 30-year sovereign debt by Japan's Finance Ministry is likely to be digested smoothly and the 30-year yield is expected to hover around 2.3%, Citi Research's Tomohisa Fujiki says in a research report. Given the Bank of Japan's schedule of JGB purchase operations, investors may also prefer yield-curve flatteners, the rates strategist adds. The JGB 30-year yield is down 1 bps at 2.315%; 10-year yield is down 0.5 bps at 1.275%. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0119 GMT - Indonesia's economic growth is expected to improve in 2025, driven by increased government spending that should strengthen domestic demand, UOB economists Enrico Tanuwidjaja and Shaun Wirawan Susanto say in a note. Investment spending may accelerate in the coming quarters, particularly in sectors like agriculture, mining and processed metals, they reckon. Higher fiscal and investment spending could boost confidence and support household consumption, they add. UOB maintains its 2025 GDP growth forecast for the country at 5.2%. Indonesia's GDP growth was 5.03% in 2024. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0025 GMT - Japanese stocks are higher as concerns about trade friction between the U.S. and its trading partners recede. Brokerage and electronics stocks are leading the gains. Nomura Holdings is up 6.6% after its nine-month net profit more than doubled. Renesas Electronics is 8.8% higher after fourth-quarter results. USD/JPY is at 152.63, compared with 153.27 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching developments in U.S. tariffs and domestic earnings. Itochu and Fujifilm Holdings are scheduled to report their quarterly earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average is up 0.9% at 39180.28. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

2345 GMT - Japanese stocks may rise as concerns about trade friction between U.S. and its trading partners recede. Nikkei futures are up 0.5% at 38950 on the SGX. USD/JPY is at 152.66, compared with 153.27 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments over U.S. tariffs as well as domestic earnings. Itochu and Fujifilm Holdings are set to announce their quarterly earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 38831.48 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

1958 GMT - Treasury yields fall again, with the 10-year dropping below 4.5% for the first time since Dec. 18, as relatively soft economic indicators retake center stage and tariff-related inflation fears ebb. ADP jobs report was stronger than expected, but the trade deficit was wider than forecast and services PMI surprisingly fell. Weekly jobless claims tomorrow are expected to indicate increasing layoffs and slowing job creation is forecast for Friday's payrolls report. The 10-year falls 0.090 percentage point to 4.421% and the two-year declines 0.030 to 4.183% (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

USD/JPY is at 152.66, compared with 153.27 as of Wednesday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are focusing on any developments over U.S. tariffs as well as domestic earnings. Itochu and Fujifilm Holdings are set to announce their quarterly earnings later in the day. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.1% to 38831.48 on Wednesday. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com)

2234 GMT - Air New Zealand looks a good bet to achieve the upper end of 1H profit guidance following a solid finish to the period, Macquarie says. Revenue per available seat kilometer, a key performance metric for airlines, rose 0.7% in 1H when currency swings and travel credits are excluded. That reversed a fall in RASK through November. Air New Zealand previously signaled expectations for a 1H pretax profit of NZ$120 million-NZ$160 million. "With a strong December set of operating statistics, we expect Air New Zealand to be at the upper end of this range," Macquarie says. "While 2H has challenges, we see stabilization rather than any further deterioration." (david.winning@wsj.com; @dwinningWSJ)

data.title: Bitcoin chart signals ‘decisive price move’ in coming weeks: Analyst
data.description:
Bitcoin could be headed for a “decisive price move” in the coming weeks as the US decides on its next Bitcoin move, amid other macroeconomic developments. 

Some analysts anticipate that direction is likely upward, as Bitcoin has yet to fully price in the US government’s pro-crypto stance.

Clearer signals on Bitcoin’s price in the coming weeks

Bitfinex analysts said in a recent markets report that Bitcoin could shift “especially as more macroeconomic developments unfold.”

They pointed out that Bitcoin has been trading within a 15% price range since mid-November when it hovered around $90,000. Historically, 15-20% consolidated price ranges tend to “resolve in either direction within 80 - 90 days.”

They said that despite Bitcoin's “high correlation” with macro conditions, its recent ability to hold above its pre-US election price of around $70,000 — despite turbulence in the crypto market triggered by US President Donald Trump imposing tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China — demonstrates the asset’s relative strength.

Trump’s tariff news led to the “largest liquidation event in crypto history” on Feb. 3, with over $2.24 billion liquidated from the crypto markets within 24 hours.

Bitcoin’s price slipped below the psychological $100,000 level, dropping to $92,584 before rebounding to $97,370 at the time of publication. However, analysts aren’t ruling out the possibility of another near-term scare event shaking Bitcoin’s price.

“While Bitcoinʼs short-term volatility may continue in response to macroeconomic influences, its long-term outlook remains positive,” they said.

Other analysts say once the US government confirms its highly anticipated Bitcoin plans, BTC could potentially clock a significant surge.

Crypto analyst Thomas Fahrer said in a Feb. 5 X post, “The day the US government announces they are buying Bitcoin, the price will go up $50,000 in a single 1-minute candle.”

Expressing similar sentiment, MN Capital founder Michaël van de Poppe said in an X post on the same day, “If there’s a case where the US government understands that it needs to become positive about the adoption of crypto, it’s now.”

“Bitcoin is still neutrally valued, where the altcoins are criminally undervalued. The entire sector is criminally undervalued,” van de Poppe said.

“The adoption is bigger than ever,” he said.

“That’s quite often not a period where you’d be saying: I’m going to log out, and I expect this market to be peaked. Far from it,” he added.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Financial Times - Feb. 6
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Financial Times. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

Headlines

- Starmer pledges UK planning reforms to boost nuclear power

- Lloyds hit with 1 billion stg tax bill after legal challenge fails

- EU probes Shein over consumer protection

- UK peers decry 'abject failure' in FCA's plan to name and shame more firms

Overview

- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer's office announced plans to expand the list of possible sites for nuclear development and propose other reforms to streamline the planning process.

- Lloyds Banking Group has been ordered to pay a 1 billion pound ($1.25 billion) tax bill after losing the initial stage of a legal dispute with HM Revenue & Customs over losses sustained in Ireland following the financial crisis.

- Fast-fashion retailer Shein will be liable for the sale of unsafe and dangerous products on their platforms, the European Commission said on Wednesday, as part of a crackdown against a flood of cheap e-commerce imports into the European Union.

- British lawmakers said that the Financial Conduct Authority should ditch already softened proposals to publicly name some companies under investigation if it cannot properly balance consumer benefits with the risks to firms and individuals.

($1 = 0.8005 pounds)

data.title: DHT Holdings Inc reports results for the quarter ended December 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• DHT Holdings Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 34 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended December 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 22 cents. The mean expectation of nine analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 19 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 16 cents to 27 cents per share.

  • Revenue fell 9.5% to $85.54 million from a year ago; analysts expected $90.58 million.

  • DHT Holdings Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 34 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $54.94 million.

  • DHT Holdings Inc shares had risen by 27.6% this quarter.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had fallen by about 39.7% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, five analysts negatively revised earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 8 "strong buy" or "buy," 1 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The average consensus recommendation for the oil & gas transportation services peer group is also "buy"

Wall Street's median 12-month price target for DHT Holdings Inc is $13.50

This summary was machine generated from LSEG data February 6 at 04:17 a.m. UTC. ​All figures in US dollars unless otherwise stated. data.title: Asian currencies rangebound; Thai inflation in line with estimates
data.description:
Key points:
  • Thai baht down 0.3%
  • AI frenzy drives Asian stocks higher
  • Jakarta stocks hit over 7-month highs

Major Asian emerging currencies traded in a tight range on Thursday against the greenback, as the shock effect of a U.S.-triggered global trade war wore off, while the Thai baht eased after domestic inflation data came in line with estimates.

Thailand's currency and the Philippine peso were trading 0.3% and 0.4% lower, respectively, while the Malaysian ringgit shed 0.1%.

Currency markets in Asia, though, appeared to be looking past the tumult in global financial markets this week caused by U.S. President Donald Trump implementing his tariff plans and China announcing retaliatory action.

"While the collective in the market remains highly cognisant that headline risk is still very much present, we see further evidence of tariff fatigue, with market players re-positioning and adding risk to portfolio exposures," said Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone.

Other currencies such as the South Korean won and Singapore dollar (SGD) were also subdued, trading 0.3% and 0.1% lower, respectively.

"There has been a series of tit-for-tat responses between the U.S. and China." said Christopher Wong, currency strategist at OCBC Bank.

"That said, responses appeared measured, and tensions are contained for now."

Thailand's headline consumer price index inched 1.32% higher in January from its year-ago levels, but was within the central bank's target range of 1% to 3% for the second consecutive month.

Indonesia said on Wednesday its economy grew at the slowest pace in the last three years and consumption was tepid, raising hopes of further stimulus measures and lower policy rates at its February 19 meeting.

The Indonesian rupiah was trading 0.3% lower while stocks in Jakarta dropped 1.6%, hitting their lowest level since June 26.

Analysts at Mandiri Sekuritas expect 2025 to be another year of stable growth in Southeast Asia's largest economy, reflecting sustained domestic demand to be offset by lower net export contribution coming from a global economic slowdown.

Asian stocks were mostly upbeat on Thursday, with export and chip-focused markets in Taiwan TWSE:TAIEX and South Korea leading the gains, tracking Wall Street moves. Shares in Taipei added 0.6% while those in Seoul were trading 0.9% higher.

Technology stocks in Taiwan led the gains across local equities after chip-major Nvidia jumped more than 5% overnight.

The rally in Taiwan stocks comes as investors continue to buy into the artificial intelligence frenzy, with MooMoo Australia market strategist Jessica Amir saying that the second AI wave is sweeping markets higher.

Markets in Singapore and Malaysia gained 0.6% and 0.1%, respectively, while shares in the Philippines fell over 1%.

HIGHLIGHTS:

** Singapore Exchange's HY profit jumps on strength in equity segments

** Indonesia's economy expands 5% in 2024

data.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank's FY24 Net Profit, Operating Income Up
data.description:
First Abu Dhabi Bank (ADX:FAB) said Thursday that its net profit and operating income for full-year 2024 rose year over year.

Net profit attributable to shareholders of the bank for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 17.06 billion Emirati dirhams, compared with 16.41 billion dirhams a year ago. EPS moved to 1.48 dirhams from 1.43 dirhams.

The Abu Dhabi-listed commercial bank's operating income was 31.63 billion dirhams, compared with 27.47 billion dirhams earlier.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a dividend of 0.75 dirham per share for the year, subject to shareholders' approval at an annual general meeting on March 11. It paid 0.71 dirham per share a year ago.

data.title: Indian bond yields inch lower; 10-year yield 15 bps above central bank policy rate
data.description:
Indian government bond yields declined slightly in early deals on Thursday, with the spread between benchmark yield and the central bank's policy rate easing to 15 basis points a day ahead of a widely-anticipated interest rate cut.

The 10-year yield (IN067934G=CC) was at 6.6520% as of 9:45 a.m. IST, compared with Wednesday's close of 6.6616%. The central bank's policy repo rate currently stands at 6.50%.

"Any upmove in yields is unlikely now till the monetary policy decision, while on the downside, we could see the benchmark yield touching around 6.62%, going into the policy decision," trader with a primary dealership said.

The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to cut interest rates for the first time in nearly five years in Governor Sanjay Malhotra's first monetary policy review. A majority of economists and market participants expect a 25 basis points cut on Friday.

Bond trading firm STCI Primary Dealer said it expects a 25 bps cut, along probability of another round of debt purchase, in a similar fashion to what was recently announced.

Hopes of a rate cut have risen since the RBI announced its mega liquidity infusion package through which it would inject around 1.50 trillion rupees ($17.14 billion) into the banking system.

The RBI has already injected around 640 billion rupees through an open market purchase of bonds and FX swap, while a 56-day repo worth 500 billion rupees is due Friday.

This would be followed by two more open market purchases of 200 billion rupees each on February 13 and February 20. Apart from this, the RBI has also bought bonds worth around 310 billion rupees from the secondary market in two weeks to January 24.

Most market participants expect the RBI's bond purchases to continue into the upcoming financial year as well. As a result, bonds are seeing increased buying interest from foreign investors as a rate action is widely getting factored in.

($1 = 87.5325 Indian rupees)
